steps.title: Rearrange to standard quadratic form
steps.content:
Starting with $kx^2 - 16x = 24x^2 - 8$, move all terms to one side:
$$kx^2 - 16x - 24x^2 + 8 = 0$$
$$(k - 24)x^2 - 16x + 8 = 0$$
This is a quadratic equation in standard form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$ with $a = k-24$, $b = -16$, and $c = 8$.

steps.title: Apply discriminant condition for two distinct real solutions
Find the greatest integer value
steps.content: Since $k$ is an integer and must satisfy $k < 32$, the greatest possible value is $k = 31$.
